msDesc.head: Astrological-astronomical and medical compilation; England, late 14th / early 15th century

supportDesc.foliation: 15th-century foliation in arabic, 1-80 (skipping two leaves with diagrams, now foliated 55b, 55c and 55 refoliated as 55a)
supportDesc.collation: Apparently 1(8)-2(8) (fols. 1-16), 3(10) (fols. 17-26) [perhaps quires d, e, f, but signatures do not survive], 4(12) (fols. 27-38, quire g), 5(8) (fols. 39-46, quire h), 6(8) (fols. 47-54, quire a), 7(8+1) (fols. 55a, 55b, 55c, 56-61: fol. 55b added; quire b), 8(8) (fols. 62-69, quire c) || 9(12-1) (fols. 70-80, 6th leaf cut out leaving a decorative zigzag pattern after fol. 75)

physDesc.accMat: Bound with a printed book: Ramon Lull, Ars brevis (Lyon, 1514)

history.provenance: Two codicological units (fols. 1-69, 70-80), together early as shown by the foliation.
Bequeathed by him to the Ashmolean Museum.
history.acquisition: Transferred to the Bodleian Library in 1860.

recordHist.source: Adapted by Matthew Holford (March 2026) from W. H. Black, A descriptive, analytical, and critical catalogue of the manuscripts bequeathed unto the University of Oxford by Elias Ashmole Esq..., Quarto Catalogues X, 1845, with additional reference to published literature as cited, and limited additional description. Quarto Catalogue, cols. 249–50 Quarto Catalogue, cols. 251–2 Summary Catalogue, vol. 2, part 2, p. 1117 Summary Catalogue, vol. 2, part 2, p. 1118

msItem.author: Alkindi
msItem.title: De planetis sub radiis
msItem.rubric: Incipit Haly de sub radiis planetarum
msItem.incipit: Saturnus in ariete sub radiis facit pluvias

listBibl.bibl: Carmody, pp. 81-2, not listing this manuscript

msItem.title: Responsum Tholomei ad sciendum utrum nativitas fuit masculina aut feminina
msItem.rubric: R' thōi ad sciendum utrum nativitas fuerit masculina aut feminina
msItem.incipit: Dicit thous hujus rei causa maxime

listBibl.bibl: Ptolmaeus Arabus et Latinus (PAL) C.2.5 ('an explanation of Quadripartitum III.6, on whether a nativity is masculine or feminine.'): listing this manuscript and Cambridge, Pembroke College MS. 204

msItem.rubric: Pronosticum Ypocratis quod dies mortis vel salutis ostendit
msItem.incipit: Saturni die qui in lectum ceciderit

msItem.author: Ps.-Hippocrates
msItem.title: Astrologia medicorum
msItem.incipit: Dixit Ypocras medicorum optimus quod medicus primo aspiciat lunam

listBibl.bibl: 'Hippocrates latinus. Repertorium of Hippocratic Writings in the Latin Middle Ages, in Traditio, XXXIII (1977), p. 287

msItem.incipit: Ut dicit Galenus in 3º. de creticis diebus luna facit in nobis 2as impressiones
Nota quod Campanus capitulo 5 compoti sui potest diem naturalem dividi in 4 quadras

msItem.title: Speculum astronomiae
msItem.incipit: Occasione quorundam librorum apud quos non est radix scientie
Que sunt magne sciencie
msItem.explicit: sed quod ambo inveniuntur ab eodem creata. Explicit
msItem.bibl: ed. Stefano Caroti et al. 1977, with this manuscript described pp. 159-162

msItem.incipit: Color eclipsis Solaris solummodo color aeris est

msItem.title: Almagesti minor V.1
msItem.incipit: Queritur primum due armille convenientis mensure

msItem.rubric: De vero motu planetarum per instrumentum
msItem.incipit: Fiat columpna et locetur
Quando domus questionis tenuerit duo signa forcius eorum est dominus ejus et significator
Cum autem dispositionem pronosticare volueris
msItem.note: According to PAL an extract from Robert Grosseteste, De impressionibus aeris

msItem.incipit: Capitulum locorum expertorum in verbis sapientum. Saturnus cum fuerit in domo sua
msItem.explicit: significat nato corporis salutem et bonam spem sui. Explicit

msItem.author: Messahalla
msItem.title: Libellus interpretationum siue de inuentione occultorum
msItem.incipit: Hec est figura extraccionis occultorum secundum regulam. Capitulum in quadrante hyles inveniuntur per eam occulta
msItem.rubric: Capitulum de occultacione anuli vel alterius rei secundum Dorotheum
msItem.incipit: (fol. 28v) Cum aliquis occultaverit
Dixit Dorotheas si fueris interrogatus de tesauro
Dixit Ptolomeus si aspexerit significator
msItem.explicit: et scito quod hoc est ex secretis astronomie et occultabant antiqui sapientes astrologorum a ceteris qui in hac arte minus docti fuerunt

listBibl.bibl: L. Thorndike, 'The Latin Translations of Astrological Works by Messahalla', Osiris, 12 (1956), 49-72 at 54-56

msItem.incipit: Cum enim aliquis in sompno pluvias et mare et flumina multocies videt

msItem.author: Ps.-Hippocrates
msItem.title: Astrologia medicorum
msItem.incipit: Ut ait Galienus sapientissimus Ypocras omnium medicorum peritissimus ait quisquis est medicus qui astrologiam ignorat
msItem.note: This differs from art. 4 only in the addition of 16 lines at the beginning, as far as the words—Jubet ergo ypocras ut omnis medicus aspiciat lunam.

msItem.author: Ps.-Dorotheus
msItem.rubric: Hic incipit Dorotheus de electionibus in horis
msItem.incipit: Hora solis in fortuna est in omni re
msItem.note: After these words—bonum est adrentare terras cavare, it is noted in the margin 'hic deficit'. Rest of fol. 38v blank
Followed by an extract from Haly Abenragel, De iudiciis astrorum (VII.100) according to PAL.

msItem.rubric: De eleccionibus per cursum lune in diversis signis
msItem.incipit: Cum luna fuerit in ariete signo firmo
msItem.explicit: Nota quod ubicunque dicitur bonum est vel cavendum est, intelligitur dum luna sit in illo signo
msItem.note: Over f. 42r is written 'Lune proprietates in signis', and over ff. 43r-44r, 'Elecciones per lunam'.

msItem.incipit: Si quis perdiderit sua domus sue et causa recuperandi pecierit oracula ut consulere sapias: hec precepta considera
Dicunt autem taliter posse conjecturare
msItem.note: Identified in PAL as Proportiones competentes in astrorum industria, c. 16, and In principio fecit Deus caelum et terram…, c. 20-22

msItem.author: Albumasar
msItem.title: Flores de electionibus
msItem.incipit: In nomine Dei incipiunt electiones quas Albumazar compilavit de confusis tractatibus antiquorum.s. qualibet die quid sit agendum quid pretermittendum secundum aspectum planetarum
msItem.note: Fols. 55(a)v and 56r are blank, and between them are two leaves (fols. 55(b), 55(c)) (fol. 55(b) folded) containing 3 circular figures. The last line is 'P.[isces] Gratum balneari. esse quietum et vacuum.'
Includes extraneous material according to PAL.

msItem.incipit: Astronomia docet de magnitudine mobili hoc est de motibus firmamenti et planetarum et de eorum effectibus
msItem.note: The titles of articles 22, 25, 31, 32, 34, are added in the margins by a different hand.

msItem.incipit: Spere sunt ix sine celi

msItem.title: Verses on the humours
msItem.incipit: Largus amans
Aries in Babilonia

msItem.title: Liber Experimentarius
msItem.incipit: Materia hujus libelli est effectus et efficacia lune et aliorum planetarum

listBibl.bibl: 'Preface of Bernardinus': C. S. F. Burnett, 'What Is the Experimentarius of Bernardus Silvestris?: A Preliminary Survey of the Material', Archives d'histoire doctrinale et littéraire du moyen âge 44 (1977) 62–108, sig. H

msItem.rubric: Quid planete agant in climatibus signorum
msItem.incipit: Cum vero saturnus ingreditur

listBibl.bibl: T. Hunt, Writing the Future - Prognostic Texts of Medieval England (2013), p. 28

msItem.incipit: Si autem quis est qui ignorat aut copiam aut paupertatem futuri anni

msItem.rubric: Signa temporum. i. serenitatis et tempestatis sumuntur a sole et luna et coloribus eorundem et a piscibus sumuntur signa
msItem.incipit: A sole
Si vero 3a. luna tonitrua audieris

msItem.rubric: Quid faces ardentes significant in celo
msItem.incipit: Nunc quoniam plura scripsi ex officijs lune temptabo quoddam explicare scriptum ex Caldea sagacitate
Ventos quatuor cardinales dicimus. Quorum primus septemtrion
Kalendas Januarij si in dominica evenerint
Quando A. fuerit litera dominicalis yemps calida
Si festum nativitatis Domini in die dominica evenerit
Si in prima nocte ventus fuerit ordinata moriuntur in illo anno
msItem.explicit: (fol. fol. 69v line 2) quia magnum donum erit in illo anno

layoutDesc.layout: The original core of fols. 1-69 appears to be fols. 47-69 (quires a-c), fully pricked and ruled for 1 col. of 30 lines, ruled space c. 120 c. 80 Fols. 39-46 are similarly pricked and ruled.

Fols. 1-38 seem to be a contemporary (or slightly later (?)) supplement, in a different hand, occasionally on parchment pricked and ruled as fols. 39-69 (fols 21-2, 27, etc.) but typically only frame-ruled.

msItem.author: William of Marseille
msItem.title: De urina non uisa
msItem.incipit: Ne ignorancie vel pocius invidie redarguar, mi Germane, qui quandoque apud Masciliam aliquando mecum studuisti
msItem.finalRubric: Explicit liber. M. Willelmi anglice nacionis de urina non visa. etc

msItem.incipit: Albaharim sane et certe sunt hore quibus declarantur mutaciones membrorum
Ptholomeus docuit nos
msItem.rubric: (fol. 74b) Sequitur figura Ptholomei quam Haly commentator suus describit sic, quando et qualiter morbi acuti habere debent suas determinaciones sive ad bonum sive ad malum. fiat ergo descripcio figure celi in inicio morbi sic
msItem.note: Most of the next page is blank, the figure not having been inserted: the text runs on to the next page.

listBibl.bibl: PAL C.3.3, "a commentary on v. 60 of the Centiloquium, dealing with the outcome of an illness according to the fixed stars of the twelve signs and other astrological parameters"
ed. G. Dell’Anna, Dies critici. La teoria della ciclicità delle patologie nel XIV secolo, Galatina, 1999, 2 vols, II, 4-7 from Cambridge, Pembroke College MS. 204.

msItem.author: Ps.-Ptolemy
msItem.title: Iudicia
msItem.incipit: Aspice in occasionibus mortem inferentibus nato
msItem.note: The same extracts are in Pembroke MS. 204, fols. 71r-73r

msItem.incipit: Quando ergo puer nascitur diligenter prospice quod signum emergat in oriente
Vidistis utiliter quod diximus dicamus itaque quas artes
msItem.explicit: in primo gradu arietis evadet casum luminis. Explicit explicit

msItem.note: Instructions for creating talismanic plates and extracts on their efficacy.

layoutDesc.layout: Frame-ruled; c. 34 long lines; ruled space c. 130 85

revisionDesc.change: James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/jamescummings/Bodleian-msDesc-ODD/blob/master/convertTolkien2Bodley.xsl
